title: Ollama lifetime management — auto-launch Ollama with the requested model
description:
The agent currently requires Ollama to already be running at http://localhost:11434 with the configured model pulled. There is no process management — the user must manually run `ollama serve` in a terminal before starting the agent. The agent should auto-launch Ollama if it's not already running, and ensure the requested model is available.

## Current State

- `OllamaProvider.cs` makes HTTP calls to `{LlmBaseUrl}/api/chat` (default http://localhost:11434)
- `ChatOptions.LlmModel` defaults to `llama3.2` (or whatever is in appsettings.json)
- If Ollama isn't running, `HttpRequestException` is caught and returns `null` — the LLM path silently fails back to the deterministic ChatInterpreter
- No process management, no health check, no model validation exists

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Add Ollama health check (startup + periodic)
File: Program.cs or new `OllamaProcessManager.cs`
- On startup, call `GET http://localhost:11434/api/tags` (Ollama's model list endpoint)
- If it responds, Ollama is running — check if configured model is in the list
- If not responding → Ollama is not running

### 2. Auto-launch Ollama if not running
- Check common Ollama install paths:
  - Windows: `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\Ollama\ollama.exe` or just `ollama` on PATH
  - Linux/Mac: `ollama` on PATH
- Launch as detached background process: `Process.Start("ollama", "serve")` with `UseShellExecute = false`
- Wait for it to be ready (poll `/api/tags` with exponential backoff, max 30s)
- Log at Information level on success, Warning on failure

### 3. Auto-pull the configured model if missing
- After Ollama is confirmed running, check `GET /api/tags` for `ChatOptions.LlmModel`
- If model not found, run `ollama pull {model}` as a child process
- Stream progress to ILogger at Information level (so dashboard shows pull progress)
- This can take minutes for large models — show progress, don't block startup entirely?
  - Option A: Block startup until model is ready (simpler, but slow first boot)
  - Option B: Start agent without LLM, pull in background, enable LLM path when ready (more complex)

### 4. Configuration
- Add to ChatOptions or a new OllamaOptions:
  ```json
  "Agent": {
    "Chat": {
      "OllamaAutoStart": true,        // auto-launch ollama serve
      "OllamaAutoPullModel": true,    // auto-pull model if missing
      "OllamaStartupTimeoutSeconds": 30  // max wait for ollama to be ready
    }
  }
  ```
- Default: `OllamaAutoStart = true`, `OllamaAutoPullModel = true` (convenience)
- Disable with `OllamaAutoStart = false` for production/manual control

### 5. Edge cases
- If Ollama is already running on a different port (non-11434) — respect `LlmBaseUrl`, don't auto-start
- If auto-start fails (ollama not installed) — log Warning, agent runs in deterministic-only mode
- If auto-pull fails (network error, disk full) — log Warning, agent runs without LLM
- Graceful shutdown: do NOT kill the Ollama process on agent exit (user may have other things using it)

## Files
- New: `Agent.Planning/Llm/OllamaProcessManager.cs` (or `WebUI.Blazor/Services/`)
- `Agent.Planning/Llm/ChatOptions.cs` — add OllamaAutoStart/OllamaAutoPullModel properties
- `WebUI.Blazor/Program.cs` — call OllamaProcessManager on startup before registering ILlmProvider
- `WebUI.Blazor/appsettings.json` — add Ollama config section

## Test Plan
- Unit test: mock Ollama HTTP responses (available/unavailable, model present/missing)
- Integration test: start with Ollama running, verify health check passes
- Integration test: start without Ollama, verify auto-launch attempts (skip on CI)

## Acceptance
- Agent logs `[ollama] checking http://localhost:11434...` on startup
- If Ollama not running, agent attempts to launch it
- If model missing, agent attempts to pull it
- Agent runs successfully without user manually launching Ollama
